demographics.living_situation: lives in a private residence; was alone at the time of the call

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I had one sip of wine an hour ago and my neck lump started burning with agonizing pain; now I'm freezing cold, shaking uncontrollably, and soaked in sweat.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The lump on my neck has been there for months but tonight it suddenly hurt terribly right after a mouthful of wine, and then I spiked a fever with drenching sweats and shaking — it came on fast and I'm scared.

symptom_narrative.onset: the neck lump appeared gradually about 3 months ago; the sudden severe pain in it started about an hour ago, straight after a small drink of wine
symptom_narrative.duration: lump for ~3 months; the acute pain, fever, shaking and sweats for the last hour
symptom_narrative.character: the lump is firm and doesn't hurt normally; tonight it suddenly burned with agonizing pain after the wine, then I went freezing cold, started shaking uncontrollably and got soaked in sweat
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the pain was excruciating; the shaking and sweats are the worst I've felt — I'm bedbound wrapped in blankets
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: a lump on the right side of my neck, above the collarbone; the fevers and sweats are all over
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: over the 3 months I've had fevers that come and go in waves, and drenching night sweats; tonight it flared badly within minutes of the wine
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: paracetamol I took a couple of hours ago barely touched the fever
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: even a small sip of alcohol triggers sharp pain right in the lump
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: constant exhausting tiredness for months, relentless itching all over my skin with no rash (I've scratched myself raw), and I've lost about a stone in weight without trying
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: no lumps or illness like this before; the lump has slowly been there for 3 months
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: usually independent, but tonight I'm bedbound and shivering under blankets

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: took some a couple of hours ago for the fever

social_history_volunteered.alcohol: only drinks occasionally; noticed that even a sip now sets off pain in the neck lump
social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: young adult, lives independently, high anxiety about the strange reaction to alcohol

objective_data_offered.timing: just now during the shaking episode
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a home thermometer

objective_data_offered.value: a visible firm lump on the right side of my neck above the collarbone, roughly the size of a golf ball
scratch marks and broken, excoriated skin over my arms and trunk from constant itching, with no rash
drenched in sweat, freezing and shaking uncontrollably, wrapped in blankets
about a stone (roughly 12 percent of my body weight) lost over the last few months without trying
